summary: One credential now meets the section-16 rotation bar: SECRET_002 (the production Postgres role password). The old password was rejected with PostgreSQL error 28P01, and the new password was revalidated by executing `select 1 as ok` successfully. No other credential has been revoked, so no other negative test exists. Per section 16, NOT_EXECUTED is never reported as PASS.

tests.interpretation: PostgreSQL error 28P01 (invalid_password) proves the old production database password is rejected by the server and is no longer an active credential.
The replacement credential established a connection through the Session pooler on port 5432 and executed the round-trip statement successfully.

not_executed: All credentials other than SECRET_002 remain unrevoked; there is no old credential to negative-test for them, and firing an authentication attempt at a still-current production credential would be a live auth attempt with no security value. Provider write access remains blocked for every other credential (see 09_DEFERRED_OR_EXCLUDED_ITEMS.json).
note: No password, complete DATABASE_URL, pooler URI containing credentials, or terminal transcript containing secret values is recorded in this artifact.
